# NOTE: This is the initial plan that was generated by an LLM and then carried out with concurrent feedback and correction from my side.

Project: Anonymous Boundary Survey

Context
Small private survey (~10 participants) for a friend group exploring personal boundaries across non-monogamy, relationship anarchy, and related topics. Anonymous, no auth, no tracking. Results stored privately on server for later local analysis.

Server environment

Shared Linux hosting, PHP available
Web root: /path/to/webroot/
Private data dir (not web-accessible): /path/to/private-data-dir/
Deployed via FTP
Survey accessible at https://your-domain.example/boundaries/


File structure
/webroot/boundaries/
    index.html       ← survey form
    submit.php       ← handles POST, writes to CSV
    thankyou.html    ← shown after submission

/private-data-dir/
    survey.csv       ← created on first submission if not exists

submit.php requirements

Accepts POST from the form
Appends one row to ../../../data/survey.csv
First row of CSV = headers (question IDs), written only if file doesn't exist yet
No IP, no timestamp, no session, no cookies — only answers
On success: redirect to thankyou.html
Use fputcsv() with file locking (LOCK_EX) to handle the edge case of concurrent writes


index.html requirements

Plain HTML, no framework, no JS required (pure form POST is fine)
Single <form method="POST" action="submit.php">
All questions are Likert scale 1–5, rendered as radio button groups
Each question has a clear label and inputs named q01 through qN
Mobile-readable, minimal styling, no external dependencies
No required fields — participants can skip questions


Question structure (to be filled in)
Each question renders as:
[Question text]
  O 1 – Strongly disagree
  O 2
  O 3
  O 4
  O 5 – Strongly agree
Questions are grouped into these categories (generate ~5 per category, ~50 total):

Time & Availability
Metamour / Network
Jealousy & Envy
Communication & Transparency
Physical & Sexual
Event & Social Attendance
Friendship & RA-adjacent
Commitments & Escalation
Personal Space & Autonomy
Emotional Capacity & Support

Frame all questions in first person, present tense, so they read as self-assessments. Example: "I feel uncomfortable when a partner makes last-minute plans with someone else." Neutral to mildly specific — nothing that implies a "correct" answer.

thankyou.html
Static page. Single sentence confirming submission. Link back to index.html in case they want to review (but not resubmit — no enforcement needed given group size).

What is explicitly out of scope

No results view
No export endpoint
No authentication
No database
No JavaScript framework
No encryption
No duplicate prevention


Deliverables

index.html with all ~50 questions
submit.php
thankyou.html
